mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to manage Argo CD clusters, enabling GitOps workflows for Kubernetes applications. It simplifies the deployment and management of Argo CD instances, making it easier to integrate continuous delivery practices into your Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes operators and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Helm repository for the Argo CD Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Argo CD Operator using Helm in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace argocd-operator

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator --version 0.17.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Argo CD Operator.
> Installs the Argo CD Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create the CatalogSource
steps.description:
This step creates a CatalogSource resource for the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

```

> Creates a CatalogSource for the Argo CD Operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This step creates an OperatorGroup resource to manage the operator's scope.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

```

> Creates an OperatorGroup for the Argo CD Operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to manage the Argo CD Operator lifecycle.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> Creates a Subscription for the Argo CD Operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Argo CD Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator

```

> Lists the pods in the argocd-operator namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Argo CD Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Argo CD applications are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator

kubectl delete namespace argocd-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Argo CD Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This step upgrades the Argo CD Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator

```

> Drains the pods in the argocd-operator namespace.
> Upgrades the Argo CD Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "CatalogSource" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OLM (Operator Lifecycle Manager) is not installed or not running.

**Fix:**
Install OLM by following the official OLM installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create subscription: catalog source not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CatalogSource resource was not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the CatalogSource YAML for errors and reapply it using kubectl.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in argocd-operator namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may have configuration issues or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the operator logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd-operator' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "OperatorGroup" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OLM is not properly configured or the API version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that OLM is installed and running correctly, and verify the API version.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved RBAC configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator pods running in the 'argocd-operator' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator' to confirm.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
